title: Jio Platforms Files DRHP, Targets ₹37,700-Crore IPO in India's Biggest-Ever Listing
summary: Jio Platforms formally filed its draft red herring prospectus with SEBI on June 19, proposing a 100% fresh issue of up to 27 crore shares at a valuation between $133–180 billion. If completed at this scale, it would be India's largest-ever stock market debut. Proceeds are earmarked to repay Reliance Jio Infocomm debt, confirming weeks of speculation that culminated in the rapid AGM-to-filing turnaround widely reported last week.

coverages.headline: Jio Platforms targets ₹37,700-crore IPO in India's biggest listing
coverages.excerpt: Jio Platforms filed its DRHP with SEBI on June 19, proposing a 100% fresh issue of up to 27 crore shares at a valuation between $133–180 billion, which would make it India's largest-ever stock market debut; proceeds earmarked to repay Reliance Jio Infocomm debt.
coverages.bias_notes: The actual DRHP filing now gives concrete numbers — a $133–180 billion valuation range and a specific 27 crore share fresh issue — replacing the more speculative '$100 billion-plus' figures and '30-minute standby' framing reported around the AGM days earlier. That a 100% fresh issue is being used to repay Reliance Jio Infocomm debt is an important structural detail: existing shareholders aren't cashing out, the capital raised goes directly to deleveraging, which is a materially different signal about company health than a pure exit-driven listing would be. The wide $47 billion span between the low and high end of the valuation range reflects genuine pricing uncertainty for an offering of unprecedented scale in the Indian market — worth treating the headline number as a range rather than a fixed figure until book-building concludes.
